params.text:
It can affect anyone

Eating disorders do not fit one picture. They affect people of every age, gender, ethnicity, body shape and background — children, older adults, men and women alike. The common belief that they only affect young, thin women is a harmful myth that leaves many people unrecognised and undiagnosed. Someone in a larger body, an older man or a person from any community can be seriously unwell.

  • Any age: from childhood through to later life.
  • Any gender: men and boys are affected too, and often overlooked.
  • Any body: you cannot tell by looking at someone.

Your supportive role

You are not expected to diagnose an eating disorder or to be an expert — that is the job of GPs and specialist eating disorder services. Your role is a supportive one: to build a trusting relationship, to notice changes in the people you support, to offer kind and consistent care, and to raise concerns with the right person so that professional help can follow.

Because eating disorders can affect anyone, it is worth staying alert with everyone in your care rather than only those who match a stereotype. Noticing early and speaking up is one of the most valuable things you can do, and it costs nothing to treat every person with the same open, non-judgemental attention.

params.question: Eating disorders only affect young women, so you do not need to consider them in older people or in men.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. Eating disorders can affect anyone, including men and older people. That belief is a harmful myth.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Eating disorders affect people of any age, gender and background — assuming otherwise leaves many people unrecognised.
